headline: Survey at 12.8% completion; Lindi dominates response but geographic spread remains weak.
collection_status: 173 of 1,350 responses collected (12.8%). Lindi accounts for 39% of all responses (68), while 3 of 10 regions have zero activity. Current pace requires acceleration to meet target within reasonable timeframe.
key_findings: Teacher participation strong: 81 responses (46.8%) are KiuFunza teachers (lower + upper), validating direct programme reach.
Expansion priority dominates: 63 stakeholders (36.4%) prioritize expanding KiuFunza over all other initiatives; policy engagement cited by 33 (19.1%) shows demand for institutionalization.
Scale readiness gap critical: Average 2.7/5 score indicates stakeholders perceive significant barriers to scaling, despite high satisfaction (4.5/5 on partner relations).
Regional imbalance acute: Lindi, Mara, Tanga, Kigoma account for 152 of 173 responses (87.9%); Songwe (8), Mtwara (12), and Singida (1) severely underrepresented.
flags: Geographic coverage collapse: 3 of 10 target regions have <1 response. Immediate outreach required to Singida, Songwe, and underperforming regions to ensure representative data.
Scale readiness paradox: Stakeholders want expansion (63) but only rate themselves 2.7/5 ready. Signals unaddressed operational, financial, or policy constraints blocking confidence.
Data quality variance: Feedback samples show mixed language and incomplete entries. Review data collection protocols to ensure consistency across regions.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted collection blitz to zero/low-response regions (Singida, Songwe, Mtwara) with 2-week sprint; simultaneously conduct 5-10 depth interviews with high-priority respondents to diagnose the readiness gap between expansion demand and confidence levels.
